opinion.h: COOK v. UNITED STATES. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE FIFTH CIRCUIT. No. 1233, Misc. Decided June 17, 1968.
opinion.p: Solicitor General Griswold, Assistant Attorney General Vinson, Beatrice Rosenberg, and Edward Fenig for the United States.
The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for a writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is reversed. Sanders v. United States, 373 U.S. 1.
MR. JUSTICE BLACK, MR. JUSTICE HARLAN, and MR. JUSTICE STEWART dissent and would deny certiorari.
392 U.S. 646 HEARD ET AL. v. RIZZO ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 1530, Misc. Decided June 17, 1968.*
[Footnote *] Together with No. 1662, Misc., Traylor et al. v. Rizzo et al., also on appeal from the same court.
Lois G. Forer for appellants in No. 1530, Misc. William Kunstler for appellants in No. 1662, Misc.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgments are affirmed.
